name: Rijāl Almaʿ Arabic

ancestors.notes: Peninsular Arabic base — most conservative; Rijāl Almaʿ is a highland/Tihāmah southwestern variety that preserves archaic features.

notes: Rijāl Almaʿ Arabic (رجال ألمع), a sedentary dialect of the ʿAsīr / Saudi Tihāmah in southwestern Saudi Arabia. Its DEFINING, and genuinely archaic, feature is that it PRESERVES THE LATERAL EMPHATICS that every other modern Arabic dialect has lost: the reflex of Old-Arabic ḍād ض is a VOICED lateralized pharyngealized (inter)dental fricative [ɮˤ] — the very lateral articulation Sībawayhi described as making Arabic lughat al-ḍād — and the reflex of ẓāʾ ظ is the corresponding VOICELESS lateralized pharyngealized fricative [ɬˤ]. Crucially the two are kept DISTINCT (Asiri 2009; Watson & Al-Azraqi 2011:427-428; Al-Azraqi 2010), where MSA and every northern dialect neutralise *ḍ and *ẓ to a single [dˤ]~[ðˤ]. This makes Rijāl Almaʿ, alongside a handful of neighbouring Tihāmah dialects (al-Rubūʿah, al-Ghayil, al-ʿIzzi) and the Modern South Arabian languages, one of the last strongholds of the Proto-Semitic lateral series. MODELED HERE: ض → [ɮˤ] (grapheme override, blocking the inherited ɮˤ→[dˤ]/[ðˤ] delateralization of the peninsular parent) and ظ → [ɬˤ]. Watson & Al-Azraqi (2011:428) note the *ḍ/*ẓ correspondence is not always clean in Rijāl Almaʿ (some *ḍ words are non-lateral, some *ẓ words take the voiced [ɮˤ]); the transcriber models the regular grapheme-conditioned mapping (ض→[ɮˤ], ظ→[ɬˤ]) and the lexical irregularities are left to a downstream lexicon. INHERITED and consistent with the southwestern highland type: qaf ق retained as [q] (peninsular default, NOT the Najdi/Bedouin [ɡ] — southwestern sedentary dialects are qaf-retaining; Asiri 2009), interdentals ث /θ/ ذ /ð/ retained, emphatic pharyngealization backing low vowels (AR_PEN_EMPH_*). NO ar-SA umbrella node: Saudi dialects do not form a pan-Saudi phonological group (the southwest patterns quite unlike Najd or the Hejaz), so ar-SA stays unassigned and this leaf parents directly to ar-x-peninsular. INPUT CONTRACT: transcribes orthography+harakat; short vowels surface only when the input is vocalised. TTS NOTE: [ɮˤ] and [ɬˤ] are rare symbols a neural frontend must have in its embedding table — the inventory-closure invariant enumerates them because this spec emits them.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E. & Al-Azraqi, M.
sources.title: Lateral fricatives and lateral emphatics in southern Saudi Arabia and Mehri
sources.publisher: Proceedings of the Seminar for Arabian Studies 41, 425-432. Archaeopress, Oxford
sources.notes: Open-access (White Rose). Documents lateral/lateralized reflexes of *ḍ and *ẓ in four Saudi Tihāmah dialects. p.427-428: Rijāl Almaʿ (Asiri 2009) has a VOICED lateralized pharyngealized interdental fricative reflex of *ḍ (transcribed [ðˤl], normalized here [ɮˤ]) and a VOICELESS lateralized pharyngealized interdental fricative reflex of *ẓ ([εl], normalized [ɬˤ]), kept distinct. Near-minimal pairs (p.428): yiḍlārib 'he fights', ṭaḍlḍlan 'she bit' (*ḍ); ḍlarf 'circumstance', εlāmī 'thirsty' (*ẓ); fāḍl 'it overfilled' (*fāḍ) vs māεl 'he churned'; tanūḍl 'she reminds' vs tamūεl 'she churns'. al-Rubūʿah younger generation contrasts voiced [ɮˤ] (*ḍ) with voiceless [ɬˤ] (*ẓ): ḍlaym 'pain', εlaym. al-Ghayil has an emphatic lateral sonorant [ɫ] (Ibn al-Jazarī's al-lām al-mufakhkhamah).

sources.author: Asiri, Y.M.
sources.title: Aspects of the phonology and morphology of Rijal Almaʿ dialect (south-west Saudi Arabia) (PhD thesis)
sources.notes: The primary monograph on Rijāl Almaʿ. Establishes the distinct lateralized reflexes of *ḍ (voiced [ɮˤ]) and *ẓ (voiceless [ɬˤ]) reported in Watson & Al-Azraqi 2011, and the southwestern sedentary consonant system (qaf-retaining, interdentals retained). Glottolog reference id 300708.

sources.author: Al-Azraqi, M.
sources.title: The ancient Ḍād in southwest Saudi Arabia
sources.notes: Documents the survival of the lateral(ized) ḍād across the ʿAsīr and Tihāmah, matching the early grammarians' description of ḍād as a lateral emphatic. Corroborates the [ɮˤ] reflex modeled here.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Emphatic pharyngealization and vowel backing, inherited from the peninsular parent.

location.notes: Rijāl Almaʿ, ʿAsīr Province, southwestern Saudi Arabia.

stress.notes: Quantity-sensitive Arabic stress, inherited from the peninsular parent; see ar-SA-x-najd for the cascade and input contract. Prosodic clitics: monosyllabic function words (negator maː, prepositions fiː/min/ʕan/ʕa/li/bi, vocative jaː, copula-negator muː/miʃ, conditional law/loː, wa-proclitic combinations) attach to a host and fall inside the host's stress domain, so they carry no word stress (Watson 2002, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP, ch.3). Pan-Arabic class; the per-lect form list reflects the function words attested in this lect. Orthographic test — a clitic homograph (e.g. verb yā 'came') is destressed in every occurrence.
